title: Serious Incident Report Pack Timeliness (Art. 73)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for Art.13.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that EU AI Act Art.13 (Transparency and information) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.21.22: Serious Incident Report Pack Timeliness (Art. 73).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.21.22' running on index=ai_incident — incident_id, milestone, _time, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686)
spl:
index=ai_incident sourcetype="ai:serious:incident" earliest=-180d
| eval m_detect=if(milestone="detected",_time,null())
| eval m_submit=if(milestone="regulator_submitted",_time,null())
| stats min(m_detect) as t0, min(m_submit) as t1 by incident_id
| eval hours_to_submit=round((t1-t0)/3600,2)
| where isnull(t1) OR hours_to_submit>72
| table incident_id, t0, t1, hours_to_submit
description: Tracks time from serious_incident_detected to regulator_packet_submitted for serious incident reporting workflows.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for EU AI Act compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ai_incident and sourcetypes ai:serious:incident. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Legal defines milestones; (2) immutable append-only index ACL; (3) alert at 60h; (4) attach packet hash in external DMS. Indexes required: `index=ai_incident`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ai:serious:incident`.
visualization: Timeline (milestones), Table (late incidents), Single value (open past 72h). Key fields: `hours_to_submit`, `incident_id`, `m_detect`, `m_submit`, `t0`, `t1`.
knownFalsePositives: High-risk system events during model retraining, scheduled validation runs, or feature rollouts that are pre-approved in the model risk register.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ai_incident` — incident_id, milestone, _time.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Legal defines milestones; (2) immutable append-only index ACL; (3) alert at 60h; (4) attach packet hash in external DMS.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ai_incident sourcetype="ai:serious:incident" earliest=-180d
| eval m_detect=if(milestone="detected",_time,null())
| eval m_submit=if(milestone="regulator_submitted",_time,null())
| stats min(m_detect) as t0, min(m_submit) as t1 by incident_id
| eval hours_to_submit=round((t1-t0)/3600,2)
| where isnull(t1) OR hours_to_submit>72
| table incident_id, t0, t1, hours_to_submit
```

Understanding this SPL

**Serious Incident Report Pack Timeliness (Art. 73) (EU AI Act)** — Tracks time from serious_incident_detected to regulator_packet_submitted for serious incident reporting workflows.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ai_incident` — incident_id, milestone, _time. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ai_incident; **sourcetype**: ai:serious:incident. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ai_incident, sourcetype="ai:serious:incident", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **m_detect** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **m_submit** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **incident_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hours_to_submit** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(t1) OR hours_to_submit>72` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: hours_to_submit, incident_id, m_detect, m_submit, t0, t1.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (milestones), Table (late incidents), Single value (open past 72h)
